jobs.summary: Job Title Senior Python/DevOps Engineer (100% Remote - USA Only) Job Description Six Feet Up is seeking a seasoned Python/DevOps Engineer (100% remote, USA only) who can work across software development, architecture, cloud infrastructure, data, AI, and client collaboration. jobs.full_offer_text: Job Title Senior Python/DevOps Engineer (100% Remote - USA Only) Job Description Six Feet Up is seeking a seasoned Python/DevOps Engineer (100% remote, USA only) who can work across software development, architecture, cloud infrastructure, data, AI, and client collaboration. This role is for an experienced engineering generalist: someone with deep expertise who is not limited to one framework, platform, or layer of the stack. You are comfortable entering unfamiliar systems, clarifying ambiguous problems, and applying sound judgment where it creates the most value. You also understand that AI has changed software engineering. You use AI-assisted development tools to accelerate research, implementation, testing, documentation, and troubleshooting while remaining accountable for the quality, security, and correctness of the result. One project may involve modernizing a mature Python application. Another may require designing an API, building a data workflow, integrating AI capabilities, or improving cloud infrastructure and observability. This role is a strong fit for someone who is curious, pragmatic, collaborative, and ready to work on the problem that matters most. Apply online: https://sixfeetup.com/company/senior-python-developer-devops-engineer-us-only Restrictions Telecommuting is OK No Agencies Please Requirements Strong candidates will have meaningful experience in several of the following areas: Python Django, Wagtail, FastAPI, Flask, or comparable frameworks REST, GraphQL, event-driven systems, and third-party integrations PostgreSQL and relational data modeling JavaScript or TypeScript and modern frontend frameworks Automated testing, code quality, and continuous integration Docker and containerized application development AWS, Azure, or another major cloud platform Kubernetes and cloud-native application delivery Infrastructure as code and deployment automation Integration of AI/ML capabilities into production systems AI-assisted development workflows Application performance, observability, and production troubleshooting Secure practices such as least-privilege access, secrets management, dependency management, and remediation of common application vulnerabilities Data ingestion, transformation, or processing workflows Authentication, authorization, SSO, and secure application design Legacy system modernization and incremental migrations As a Senior Python/DevOps Engineer, you will: Work with clients and teammates to understand business goals, user needs, constraints, and desired outcomes. Design and deliver secure, maintainable, well-tested software across application, data, integration, and infrastructure boundaries. Use AI tools responsibly to accelerate development while reviewing and validating generated code, tests, analysis, and recommendations. Rapidly evaluate emerging technologies and determine where they can improve delivery, quality, security, or developer productivity. Help evolve how the team uses AI for coding, testing, code review, documentation, debugging, estimation, discovery, and delivery. Work with delivery leads to turn unclear initiatives into practical, incremental technical plans. Identify architectural, security, privacy, performance, reliability, and delivery risks early. Contribute across the application stack, including backend services, APIs, databases, integrations, frontend applications, data workflows, and cloud infrastructure. Improve testing, deployment automation, observability, documentation, and developer workflows. You are an experienced software engineer who: Learns publicly by sharing findings, examples, warnings, and practical guidance with teammates. Can solve problems that cross traditional boundaries between development, data, infrastructure, and operations. Makes decisions based on client needs, project constraints, maintainability, risk, and long-term value. Communicates progress, uncertainty, risks, and blockers openly. Treats learning, experimentation, and technical reinvention as part of the job, not something reserved for spare time. Is energized by rapidly changing technology and comfortable replacing familiar workflows when a better approach emerges. Learns publicly by sharing discoveries, failures, limitations, and useful techniques with the team through demos, documentation, pairing, and technical discussions. You are successful when you: Understand our clients’ problems and aspirations. Proactively seek and provide actionable feedback from and to peers. Are reliable in delivering work and escalate issues quickly when needed. Promote best practices while following company methodologies to ensure quality and stability. About the Company Six Feet Up makes the world a better place by using technology to accelerate the initiatives of companies that do good. Our engineers specialize in app development, AI, big data and cloud technology. Our impactful clients focus on tech & science, social good, and the environment. Contact Info E-mail contact : marketing @ sixfeetup . com Web : https://sixfeetup.com/company/senior-python-developer-devops-engineer-us-only
jobs.full_offer_html:
Job Title

Senior Python/DevOps Engineer (100% Remote - USA Only)

Job Description

Six Feet Up is seeking a seasoned Python/DevOps Engineer (100% remote, USA only) who can work across software development, architecture, cloud infrastructure, data, AI, and client collaboration.

This role is for an experienced engineering generalist: someone with deep expertise who is not limited to one framework, platform, or layer of the stack. You are comfortable entering unfamiliar systems, clarifying ambiguous problems, and applying sound judgment where it creates the most value.

You also understand that AI has changed software engineering. You use AI-assisted development tools to accelerate research, implementation, testing, documentation, and troubleshooting while remaining accountable for the quality, security, and correctness of the result.

One project may involve modernizing a mature Python application. Another may require designing an API, building a data workflow, integrating AI capabilities, or improving cloud infrastructure and observability. This role is a strong fit for someone who is curious, pragmatic, collaborative, and ready to work on the problem that matters most.

Apply online: https://sixfeetup.com/company/senior-python-developer-devops-engineer-us-only

Restrictions

  • Telecommuting is OK
  • No Agencies Please

Requirements

Strong candidates will have meaningful experience in several of the following areas:

  • Python
  • Django, Wagtail, FastAPI, Flask, or comparable frameworks
  • REST, GraphQL, event-driven systems, and third-party integrations
  • PostgreSQL and relational data modeling
  • JavaScript or TypeScript and modern frontend frameworks
  • Automated testing, code quality, and continuous integration
  • Docker and containerized application development
  • AWS, Azure, or another major cloud platform
  • Kubernetes and cloud-native application delivery
  • Infrastructure as code and deployment automation
  • Integration of AI/ML capabilities into production systems
  • AI-assisted development workflows
  • Application performance, observability, and production troubleshooting
  • Secure practices such as least-privilege access, secrets management, dependency management, and remediation of common application vulnerabilities
  • Data ingestion, transformation, or processing workflows
  • Authentication, authorization, SSO, and secure application design
  • Legacy system modernization and incremental migrations

As a Senior Python/DevOps Engineer, you will:

  • Work with clients and teammates to understand business goals, user needs, constraints, and desired outcomes.
  • Design and deliver secure, maintainable, well-tested software across application, data, integration, and infrastructure boundaries.
  • Use AI tools responsibly to accelerate development while reviewing and validating generated code, tests, analysis, and recommendations.
  • Rapidly evaluate emerging technologies and determine where they can improve delivery, quality, security, or developer productivity.
  • Help evolve how the team uses AI for coding, testing, code review, documentation, debugging, estimation, discovery, and delivery.
  • Work with delivery leads to turn unclear initiatives into practical, incremental technical plans.
  • Identify architectural, security, privacy, performance, reliability, and delivery risks early.
  • Contribute across the application stack, including backend services, APIs, databases, integrations, frontend applications, data workflows, and cloud infrastructure.
  • Improve testing, deployment automation, observability, documentation, and developer workflows.

You are an experienced software engineer who:

  • Learns publicly by sharing findings, examples, warnings, and practical guidance with teammates.
  • Can solve problems that cross traditional boundaries between development, data, infrastructure, and operations.
  • Makes decisions based on client needs, project constraints, maintainability, risk, and long-term value.
  • Communicates progress, uncertainty, risks, and blockers openly.
  • Treats learning, experimentation, and technical reinvention as part of the job, not something reserved for spare time.
  • Is energized by rapidly changing technology and comfortable replacing familiar workflows when a better approach emerges.
  • Learns publicly by sharing discoveries, failures, limitations, and useful techniques with the team through demos, documentation, pairing, and technical discussions.

You are successful when you:

  1. Understand our clients’ problems and aspirations.
  2. Proactively seek and provide actionable feedback from and to peers.
  3. Are reliable in delivering work and escalate issues quickly when needed.
  4. Promote best practices while following company methodologies to ensure quality and stability.

About the Company

Six Feet Up makes the world a better place by using technology to accelerate the initiatives of companies that do good. Our engineers specialize in app development, AI, big data and cloud technology. Our impactful clients focus on tech & science, social good, and the environment.

Contact Info

  • E-mail contact: marketing@sixfeetup.com
  • Web: https://sixfeetup.com/company/senior-python-developer-devops-engineer-us-only
jobs.dedupe_key: senior python devops engineer 100 remote usa only|six feet up

jobs.title: Python + TypeScript Engineers, Fusionbox
jobs.location: Remote (US Based), Any US State, US
jobs.summary: Job Title Python + TypeScript Engineers Job Description Fusionbox is a software engineering consultancy that builds enterprise software the right way. We negotiate the right to open source all our client code, maintain a handful of popular libraries, and our engineers contribute to Django core. We sponsor PyCon, DjangoCon, and Django Girls because we're invested in the ecosystem we build on. We're looking for software engineers who value software design, system architecture, and collaboration. Y
jobs.full_offer_text: Job Title Python + TypeScript Engineers Job Description Fusionbox is a software engineering consultancy that builds enterprise software the right way. We negotiate the right to open source all our client code, maintain a handful of popular libraries, and our engineers contribute to Django core. We sponsor PyCon, DjangoCon, and Django Girls because we're invested in the ecosystem we build on. We're looking for software engineers who value software design, system architecture, and collaboration. You should be strong in most of these areas: web application security, relational database design (PostgreSQL query plans, indexes, and constraints, not whatever the ORM emits), Django internals, React, and distributed systems. What you'll actually work on: complex state machines for financial workflows, multi-tenant architectures with row-level security, custom database functions when the ORM isn't enough, and React frontends that handle real-time data without being a mess of useEffect race conditions. A note on how we work: we think deeply about the systems we build, and we believe understanding comes from doing the work yourself, not from handing it to a model. If you ship code you don't understand, we're genuinely not a fit, and that's fine. If that paragraph made you nod, we want to hear from you. We're a small team where you'll own features end-to-end (database design to React components) and have opportunities to shape technical decisions. You'll work with a team that practices empathy and values work-life balance. If you match these requirements and wish your job involved more variety and less bureaucracy, let's talk: info@fusionbox.com . Restrictions Telecommuting is OK No Agencies Please Requirements 5 years of using Python pythonically 3 years of React Experience Django Experience Preferred US-Based Must be able to communicate complex technical concepts clearly and professionally in spoken English. About the Company We believe the best software comes from first understanding the problem it tries to solve. We ask a lot of questions, invest time in learning our clients' businesses and domains, and work to understand the challenges from their perspective. We are a team of senior-level software engineers who are happy with the variety of software challenges our clients bring to us. We believe in design for design's sake and we hire people who are passionate about their work. We know that well-supported people produce their best work, so we have an environment where our team can pursue their interests, spend time with family and friends, and keep a happy balance. Contact Info Contact : Ivy Hastings E-mail contact : info @ fusionbox . com Web : https://www.fusionbox.com
jobs.full_offer_html:
Job Title

Python + TypeScript Engineers

Job Description

Fusionbox is a software engineering consultancy that builds enterprise software the right way. We negotiate the right to open source all our client code, maintain a handful of popular libraries, and our engineers contribute to Django core. We sponsor PyCon, DjangoCon, and Django Girls because we're invested in the ecosystem we build on.

We're looking for software engineers who value software design, system architecture, and collaboration. You should be strong in most of these areas: web application security, relational database design (PostgreSQL query plans, indexes, and constraints, not whatever the ORM emits), Django internals, React, and distributed systems. What you'll actually work on: complex state machines for financial workflows, multi-tenant architectures with row-level security, custom database functions when the ORM isn't enough, and React frontends that handle real-time data without being a mess of useEffect race conditions.

A note on how we work: we think deeply about the systems we build, and we believe understanding comes from doing the work yourself, not from handing it to a model. If you ship code you don't understand, we're genuinely not a fit, and that's fine. If that paragraph made you nod, we want to hear from you.

We're a small team where you'll own features end-to-end (database design to React components) and have opportunities to shape technical decisions. You'll work with a team that practices empathy and values work-life balance.

If you match these requirements and wish your job involved more variety and less bureaucracy, let's talk: info@fusionbox.com.

Restrictions

  • Telecommuting is OK
  • No Agencies Please

Requirements

  • 5 years of using Python pythonically
  • 3 years of React Experience
  • Django Experience Preferred
  • US-Based
  • Must be able to communicate complex technical concepts clearly and professionally in spoken English.

About the Company

We believe the best software comes from first understanding the problem it tries to solve. We ask a lot of questions, invest time in learning our clients' businesses and domains, and work to understand the challenges from their perspective.

We are a team of senior-level software engineers who are happy with the variety of software challenges our clients bring to us. We believe in design for design's sake and we hire people who are passionate about their work. We know that well-supported people produce their best work, so we have an environment where our team can pursue their interests, spend time with family and friends, and keep a happy balance.

Contact Info

  • Contact: Ivy Hastings
  • E-mail contact: info@fusionbox.com
  • Web: https://www.fusionbox.com

jobs.full_offer_html:
Job Title

Senior Python Engineer

Job Description

We are seeking a Senior Python Engineer who brings strong backend development expertise along with a solid grasp of cloud-native solutions. The right candidate will be capable of designing, building and maintaining scalable backend services through Python and modern cloud platforms.

Countries : Argentina, Australia, Bulgaria, Canada, Chile, China, Colombia, Czech Republic, Greece, Hong Kong, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico,Romania, Singapore, Slovakia, Ukraine, USA, Vietnam

Restrictions

  • Telecommuting is OK
  • Agencies are OK

Requirements

Requirements

  • Solid experience in Python backend development
  • Familiarity with frameworks like FastAPI, Flask or Django
  • Practical experience using AWS, Azure or GCP
  • Proven background building REST APIs and microservices
  • Sound knowledge of relational and/or NoSQL databases
  • Exposure to CI/CD, testing and modern software engineering practices
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities

Nice to have

  • Working knowledge of Docker and Kubernetes
  • Background in serverless architectures
  • Familiarity with data pipelines or event-driven architectures
  • Understanding of Terraform or infrastructure as code

We offer/Benefits

  • Connectivity Bonus (25,000 ARS are paid with a salary receipt at the end of each month as a non-wages concept).

About the Company

EPAM is a leading global provider of digital platform engineering and development services. We are committed to having a positive impact on our customers, our employees, and our communities. We embrace a dynamic and inclusive culture. Here you will collaborate with multi-national teams, contribute to a myriad of innovative projects that deliver the most creative and cutting-edge solutions, and have an opportunity to continuously learn and grow. No matter where you are located, you will join a dedicated, creative, and diverse community that will help you discover your fullest potential.

Contact Info

  • Contact: Abhishek
  • E-mail contact: hiring@black.com
  • Web: https://www.epam.com/

jobs.title: Founding ML/Data Scientist (Remote, UK), MyDataValue
jobs.summary: Job Title Founding ML/Data Scientist (Remote, UK) Job Description We're an AI-driven hospitality tech platform helping vacation rental owners automate their revenue on Booking.com and Airbnb. We sit on Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing across thousands of properties — and right now we barely use it. Our agent is rules-based where it should be data-driven, and our promotions engine has no model behind it. We're looking for a founding ML/Data Scient
jobs.full_offer_text: Job Title Founding ML/Data Scientist (Remote, UK) Job Description We're an AI-driven hospitality tech platform helping vacation rental owners automate their revenue on Booking.com and Airbnb. We sit on Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing across thousands of properties — and right now we barely use it. Our agent is rules-based where it should be data-driven, and our promotions engine has no model behind it. We're looking for a founding ML/Data Scientist to change that. You'll turn dormant data into a smarter agent and a compounding moat. The Role You'll be the first person to seriously dig into our data, find the signals that drive revenue, and work with engineering to make the agent act on them. Find what matters — dig through Booking.com, Airbnb, and competitive data to separate signal from noise. Separate cause from correlation — when a promotion drives a booking, was it causal? When occupancy drops, is it pricing, seasonality, or competition? We want rigorous answers, not guesses. Turn insights into revenue — translate findings into logic the agent can use, measurably better than a human's. Shape the data strategy — decide what to track, what to enrich, and where the highest-value opportunities are. Ship to production — this isn't a notebooks role. You'll work inside our Django + ClickHouse stack alongside the engineering team. Logistics Remote-first for UK-based candidates. Expect to be in London for WeWork sessions a few days a month, flexibly, for onboarding, planning, and product work. Interview Process 1. 30-min non-technical call with the CTO Take-home data challenge using a sample of real OTA data 1-hour deep dive on your submission with the founding team In-person meeting with both founders Offer Why This Role Unique data — Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing. This dataset doesn't exist at most companies. Real impact — your models drive revenue decisions for thousands of properties. High equity for our stage. A compounding moat — every property we add makes the data richer and the models better. Salary £70k–£100k · Equity 0.5%–1%. Restrictions Telecommuting is OK No Agencies Please Requirements Who You Are A real data scientist who reaches for statistics and experiments first, and knows when a result is significant versus noise. Strong with time series. Causal by instinct — you don't confuse correlation with causation. You can design holdouts and A/B tests, and reason about confounders when a clean experiment isn't possible. Comfortable in the mess — real OTA data is dirty, sparse, and seasonal. You wrangle it, find the signal, and build features that predict revenue. Fluent in modelling — forecasting, uplift and causal models, and the judgement to pick the simplest thing that works. Ships to production — not a notebooks role. You write clean, deployable Python (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) and get your models live in our Django and ClickHouse stack. A product thinker who cares about revenue impact, not what's technically interesting. AI-first — you use AI coding tools with multiple agents to ship faster, ideally pushing into self-learning ML or agentic workflows. If you don't already use frontier AI tools, this isn't the role for you. Skills: Python · pandas · NumPy · scikit-learn · Time Series · Causal Inference · Forecasting · Experimentation · PostgreSQL · ClickHouse · Django · AI agents About the Company MyDataValue is an AI-driven hospitality tech platform that automates revenue for short-term rental operators and independent hotels across Booking.com and Airbnb. We've built private API access, channel integrations, and competitive pricing data across thousands of properties into an agent that optimises pricing, promotions, and visibility automatically. London-based, founder-led, and venture-backed, with strong revenue, near-zero churn, and a fast-growing client base. Contact Info Contact : Martin E-mail contact : martin @ mydatavalue . com Web : https://www.mydatavalue.com/founding-ml-data-engineer/
jobs.full_offer_html:
Job Title

Founding ML/Data Scientist (Remote, UK)

Job Description

We're an AI-driven hospitality tech platform helping vacation rental owners automate their revenue on Booking.com and Airbnb.

We sit on Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing across thousands of properties — and right now we barely use it. Our agent is rules-based where it should be data-driven, and our promotions engine has no model behind it. We're looking for a founding ML/Data Scientist to change that. You'll turn dormant data into a smarter agent and a compounding moat.

The Role

You'll be the first person to seriously dig into our data, find the signals that drive revenue, and work with engineering to make the agent act on them.

  • Find what matters — dig through Booking.com, Airbnb, and competitive data to separate signal from noise.
  • Separate cause from correlation — when a promotion drives a booking, was it causal? When occupancy drops, is it pricing, seasonality, or competition? We want rigorous answers, not guesses.
  • Turn insights into revenue — translate findings into logic the agent can use, measurably better than a human's.
  • Shape the data strategy — decide what to track, what to enrich, and where the highest-value opportunities are.
  • Ship to production — this isn't a notebooks role. You'll work inside our Django + ClickHouse stack alongside the engineering team.

Logistics Remote-first for UK-based candidates. Expect to be in London for WeWork sessions a few days a month, flexibly, for onboarding, planning, and product work.

Interview Process 1. 30-min non-technical call with the CTO

  1. Take-home data challenge using a sample of real OTA data
  2. 1-hour deep dive on your submission with the founding team
  3. In-person meeting with both founders
  4. Offer

Why This Role

  • Unique data — Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing. This dataset doesn't exist at most companies.
  • Real impact — your models drive revenue decisions for thousands of properties.
  • High equity for our stage.
  • A compounding moat — every property we add makes the data richer and the models better.

Salary £70k–£100k · Equity 0.5%–1%.

Restrictions

  • Telecommuting is OK
  • No Agencies Please

Requirements

Who You Are

  • A real data scientist who reaches for statistics and experiments first, and knows when a result is significant versus noise. Strong with time series.
  • Causal by instinct — you don't confuse correlation with causation. You can design holdouts and A/B tests, and reason about confounders when a clean experiment isn't possible.
  • Comfortable in the mess — real OTA data is dirty, sparse, and seasonal. You wrangle it, find the signal, and build features that predict revenue.
  • Fluent in modelling — forecasting, uplift and causal models, and the judgement to pick the simplest thing that works.
  • Ships to production — not a notebooks role. You write clean, deployable Python (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) and get your models live in our Django and ClickHouse stack.
  • A product thinker who cares about revenue impact, not what's technically interesting.
  • AI-first — you use AI coding tools with multiple agents to ship faster, ideally pushing into self-learning ML or agentic workflows. If you don't already use frontier AI tools, this isn't the role for you.

Skills: Python · pandas · NumPy · scikit-learn · Time Series · Causal Inference · Forecasting · Experimentation · PostgreSQL · ClickHouse · Django · AI agents

About the Company

MyDataValue is an AI-driven hospitality tech platform that automates revenue for short-term rental operators and independent hotels across Booking.com and Airbnb. We've built private API access, channel integrations, and competitive pricing data across thousands of properties into an agent that optimises pricing, promotions, and visibility automatically. London-based, founder-led, and venture-backed, with strong revenue, near-zero churn, and a fast-growing client base.

Contact Info

  • Contact: Martin
  • E-mail contact: martin@mydatavalue.com
  • Web: https://www.mydatavalue.com/founding-ml-data-engineer/

jobs.title: Freelance Full-Stack Web App Developer
jobs.summary: Mindrift is looking for skilled Full-Stack Web App Developers (JavaScript/TypeScript + Python or Node) to join the Tendem project ( Tendem.ai ) and build interactive browser-based applications in a hybrid AI + human environment. The role (“AI Pilot”) involves collaborating with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while the developer provides full-stack engineering expertise, architectural judgment, and quality control to ensure web apps are functional, robust, and ready for real users. R
jobs.full_offer_text: Mindrift is looking for skilled Full-Stack Web App Developers (JavaScript/TypeScript + Python or Node) to join the Tendem project ( Tendem.ai ) and build interactive browser-based applications in a hybrid AI + human environment. The role (“AI Pilot”) involves collaborating with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while the developer provides full-stack engineering expertise, architectural judgment, and quality control to ensure web apps are functional, robust, and ready for real users. Responsibilities include: designing, building, and refining interactive browser-based applications with real logic, state, persistence, and user input; developing frontend apps using modern frameworks (React, Next.js, Vue or similar) and backend APIs (Python/FastAPI/Flask or Node/Express); implementing data models/schemas/persistence using SQL (PostgreSQL, SQLite) or NoSQL; adding authentication, sessions, and role-based access where needed; integrating third-party APIs and AI/LLM services (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.); managing state, validation, error and loading states; building standalone Python tools and scripts for data processing, API clients, or lightweight utilities; evaluating AI-generated code and refactoring for correctness, security, performance, and maintainability; and writing clear, testable code while debugging end-to-end issues across frontend, backend, and database. Requirements/experience: at least 3 years of relevant full-stack development experience; strong foundation across frontend frameworks, backend architecture, interactive browser-based apps shipped end-to-end; strong command of JavaScript/TypeScript and a modern frontend framework; solid backend experience in Python (FastAPI, Flask, Django) and/or Node (Express, NestJS); relational database experience (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite) and basic schema design; REST API development with validation, error handling, and authentication flows; familiarity with deployment platforms (Vercel, Netlify, Render, Fly.io , Railway, etc.); experience integrating LLM APIs is a strong plus; comfort with Git and basic testing; attention to detail and commitment to production-ready work; self-directed ability to architect/build/ship features; portfolio of shipped web applications; English proficiency at B2+. Workload: part-time freelance with estimated 10–20 hours/week during active phases.
jobs.full_offer_html:
Mindrift is looking for skilled Full-Stack Web App Developers (JavaScript/TypeScript + Python or Node) to join the Tendem project (Tendem.ai) and build interactive browser-based applications in a hybrid AI + human environment. The role (“AI Pilot”) involves collaborating with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while the developer provides full-stack engineering expertise, architectural judgment, and quality control to ensure web apps are functional, robust, and ready for real users.

Responsibilities include: designing, building, and refining interactive browser-based applications with real logic, state, persistence, and user input; developing frontend apps using modern frameworks (React, Next.js, Vue or similar) and backend APIs (Python/FastAPI/Flask or Node/Express); implementing data models/schemas/persistence using SQL (PostgreSQL, SQLite) or NoSQL; adding authentication, sessions, and role-based access where needed; integrating third-party APIs and AI/LLM services (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.); managing state, validation, error and loading states; building standalone Python tools and scripts for data processing, API clients, or lightweight utilities; evaluating AI-generated code and refactoring for correctness, security, performance, and maintainability; and writing clear, testable code while debugging end-to-end issues across frontend, backend, and database.

Requirements/experience: at least 3 years of relevant full-stack development experience; strong foundation across frontend frameworks, backend architecture, interactive browser-based apps shipped end-to-end; strong command of JavaScript/TypeScript and a modern frontend framework; solid backend experience in Python (FastAPI, Flask, Django) and/or Node (Express, NestJS); relational database experience (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite) and basic schema design; REST API development with validation, error handling, and authentication flows; familiarity with deployment platforms (Vercel, Netlify, Render, Fly.io, Railway, etc.); experience integrating LLM APIs is a strong plus; comfort with Git and basic testing; attention to detail and commitment to production-ready work; self-directed ability to architect/build/ship features; portfolio of shipped web applications; English proficiency at B2+.

Workload: part-time freelance with estimated 10–20 hours/week during active phases.

jobs.title: Senior Python/Django Developer

Founded in 2010 and headquartered in Zürich, we connect businesses with over 1,500 verified suppliers across 50+ product and service categories — from IT services and insurance to HR and marketing. jobs.full_offer_text: Gryps AG is Switzerland's leading procurement platform for SMEs. Founded in 2010 and headquartered in Zürich, we connect businesses with over 1,500 verified suppliers across 50+ product and service categories — from IT services and insurance to HR and marketing. Where we are now: We're investing in platform modernization. We're improving our operational excellence through better data foundations, smarter automations, and modern tooling — reducing manual work and enabling the business to scale. The Project The platform is a mature Django application serving the Swiss SME market. We're modernizing it — improving the architecture, data foundations, and tooling so the business can scale and the platform can evolve. What this means: Improve and extend backend capabilities to support modern interfaces and integrations Help define the technical direction as the architecture evolves Keep the core stable — no big-bang rewrite, thoughtful evolution You'll own backend architecture decisions for small development team. Drive backend modernization — evolve the Django application architecture, build APIs, improve data layer Own Salesforce integration — bidirectional sync via django-salesforce, SOQL queries, data model alignment Shape technical direction — evaluate approaches, define patterns, make architecture decisions Lead technical decisions for the backend team, code reviews, architecture discussions Maintain the existing stack — Django/PostgreSQL/Celery/Redis while the platform evolves Support CRM integration work — build interfaces for the CRM platform to trigger Django actions Required Experience (5+ years) You've built and maintained production Django applications at scale — not just used the framework, but understood its internals: ORM optimization, middleware, signals, class-based views. You're comfortable with: Python & Django — Modern Python (type hints, async), Django ORM, custom middleware, signals Django REST Framework — Serializers, ViewSets, custom permissions, nested relationships PostgreSQL — Complex queries, performance tuning, and ideally PostGIS for spatial/location-based work Celery + Redis/RabbitMQ — Async task processing, scheduled jobs, distributed queues Git/GitLab — CI/CD pipelines, merge request workflows, code review culture Docker & Cloud platforms — Containerized development Nice to Have Salesforce API integration (django-salesforce or similar bidirectional CRM sync) DjangoCMS Multi-language application experience (German/French/Italian) Who You Are Self-directed: You don't wait for detailed specs. Given a goal, you figure out how to get there. You're comfortable working in a growing team where not everything is documented yet. Clear communicator: This is an async-first environment. You write things down, explain decisions, and ask good questions in text. Your English is strong enough for daily written communication and regular video syncs. Pragmatic: You ship working code. You care about quality — clean, testable code, proper reviews — but you know when to move fast and when to slow down. Collaborative: You work effectively across time zones and functions — with product, operations, and other developers. You're used to agile workflows and don't need someone managing your day. How to Apply Send your application with the following: Your CV and a short motivation letter A link to your GitHub profile or portfolio
jobs.full_offer_html:
Gryps AG is Switzerland's leading procurement platform for SMEs. Founded in 2010 and headquartered in Zürich, we connect businesses with over 1,500 verified suppliers across 50+ product and service categories — from IT services and insurance to HR and marketing.

Where we are now: We're investing in platform modernization. We're improving our operational excellence through better data foundations, smarter automations, and modern tooling — reducing manual work and enabling the business to scale.

The Project The platform is a mature Django application serving the Swiss SME market. We're modernizing it — improving the architecture, data foundations, and tooling so the business can scale and the platform can evolve.

What this means:

  • Improve and extend backend capabilities to support modern interfaces and integrations
  • Help define the technical direction as the architecture evolves
  • Keep the core stable — no big-bang rewrite, thoughtful evolution

You'll own backend architecture decisions for small development team.

  • Drive backend modernization — evolve the Django application architecture, build APIs, improve data layer
  • Own Salesforce integration — bidirectional sync via django-salesforce, SOQL queries, data model alignment
  • Shape technical direction — evaluate approaches, define patterns, make architecture decisions
  • Lead technical decisions for the backend team, code reviews, architecture discussions
  • Maintain the existing stack — Django/PostgreSQL/Celery/Redis while the platform evolves
  • Support CRM integration work — build interfaces for the CRM platform to trigger Django actions

Required Experience (5+ years) You've built and maintained production Django applications at scale — not just used the framework, but understood its internals: ORM optimization, middleware, signals, class-based views. You're comfortable with:

  • Python & Django — Modern Python (type hints, async), Django ORM, custom middleware, signals
  • Django REST Framework — Serializers, ViewSets, custom permissions, nested relationships
  • PostgreSQL — Complex queries, performance tuning, and ideally PostGIS for spatial/location-based work
  • Celery + Redis/RabbitMQ — Async task processing, scheduled jobs, distributed queues
  • Git/GitLab — CI/CD pipelines, merge request workflows, code review culture
  • Docker & Cloud platforms — Containerized development

Nice to Have

  • Salesforce API integration (django-salesforce or similar bidirectional CRM sync)
  • DjangoCMS
  • Multi-language application experience (German/French/Italian)

Who You Are

  • Self-directed: You don't wait for detailed specs. Given a goal, you figure out how to get there. You're comfortable working in a growing team where not everything is documented yet.
  • Clear communicator: This is an async-first environment. You write things down, explain decisions, and ask good questions in text. Your English is strong enough for daily written communication and regular video syncs.
  • Pragmatic: You ship working code. You care about quality — clean, testable code, proper reviews — but you know when to move fast and when to slow down.
  • Collaborative: You work effectively across time zones and functions — with product, operations, and other developers. You're used to agile workflows and don't need someone managing your day.

How to Apply Send your application with the following:

  • Your CV and a short motivation letter
  • A link to your GitHub profile or portfolio

jobs.title: Founding ML/Data Scientist (Remote, UK)
jobs.location: Remote, Remote - London UK, United Kingdom
jobs.summary: Job Description Unique data — Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing. This dataset doesn't exist at most companies. Real impact — your models drive revenue decisions for thousands of properties. High equity for our stage. A compounding moat — every property we add makes the data richer and the models better. Salary £70k–£100k · Equity 0.5%–1%. Restrictions Telecommuting is OK No Agencies Please Requirements A real data scientist who reaches for statist
jobs.full_offer_text: Job Description Unique data — Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing. This dataset doesn't exist at most companies. Real impact — your models drive revenue decisions for thousands of properties. High equity for our stage. A compounding moat — every property we add makes the data richer and the models better. Salary £70k–£100k · Equity 0.5%–1%. Restrictions Telecommuting is OK No Agencies Please Requirements A real data scientist who reaches for statistics and experiments first, and knows when a result is significant versus noise. Strong with time series. Causal by instinct — you don't confuse correlation with causation. You can design holdouts and A/B tests, and reason about confounders when a clean experiment isn't possible. Comfortable in the mess — real OTA data is dirty, sparse, and seasonal. You wrangle it, find the signal, and build features that predict revenue. Fluent in modelling — forecasting, uplift and causal models, and the judgement to pick the simplest thing that works. Ships to production — not a notebooks role. You write clean, deployable Python (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) and get your models live in our Django and ClickHouse stack. A product thinker who cares about revenue impact, not what's technically interesting. AI-first — you use AI coding tools with multiple agents to ship faster, ideally pushing into self-learning ML or agentic workflows. If you don't already use frontier AI tools, this isn't the role for you. Skills: Python · pandas · NumPy · scikit-learn · Time Series · Causal Inference · Forecasting · Experimentation · PostgreSQL · ClickHouse · Django · AI agents About the Company MyDataValue is an AI-driven hospitality tech platform that automates revenue for short-term rental operators and independent hotels across Booking.com and Airbnb. We've built private API access, channel integrations, and competitive pricing data across thousands of properties into an agent that optimises pricing, promotions, and visibility automatically. London-based, founder-led, and venture-backed, with strong revenue, near-zero churn, and a fast-growing client base. Contact Info Contact: Martin E-mail contact: [email protected] Web: https://www.mydatavalue.com/founding-ml-data-engineer/
jobs.full_offer_html:
Job Description

  • Unique data — Booking.com private API data, Airbnb channel data, and scraped competitive pricing. This dataset doesn't exist at most companies.
  • Real impact — your models drive revenue decisions for thousands of properties.
  • High equity for our stage.
  • A compounding moat — every property we add makes the data richer and the models better.

Salary £70k–£100k · Equity 0.5%–1%.

Restrictions

  • Telecommuting is OK
  • No Agencies Please

Requirements

  • A real data scientist who reaches for statistics and experiments first, and knows when a result is significant versus noise. Strong with time series.
  • Causal by instinct — you don't confuse correlation with causation. You can design holdouts and A/B tests, and reason about confounders when a clean experiment isn't possible.
  • Comfortable in the mess — real OTA data is dirty, sparse, and seasonal. You wrangle it, find the signal, and build features that predict revenue.
  • Fluent in modelling — forecasting, uplift and causal models, and the judgement to pick the simplest thing that works.
  • Ships to production — not a notebooks role. You write clean, deployable Python (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) and get your models live in our Django and ClickHouse stack.
  • A product thinker who cares about revenue impact, not what's technically interesting.
  • AI-first — you use AI coding tools with multiple agents to ship faster, ideally pushing into self-learning ML or agentic workflows. If you don't already use frontier AI tools, this isn't the role for you.

Skills: Python · pandas · NumPy · scikit-learn · Time Series · Causal Inference · Forecasting · Experimentation · PostgreSQL · ClickHouse · Django · AI agents

About the Company

MyDataValue is an AI-driven hospitality tech platform that automates revenue for short-term rental operators and independent hotels across Booking.com and Airbnb. We've built private API access, channel integrations, and competitive pricing data across thousands of properties into an agent that optimises pricing, promotions, and visibility automatically. London-based, founder-led, and venture-backed, with strong revenue, near-zero churn, and a fast-growing client base.

Contact Info

  • Contact: Martin
  • E-mail contact: [email protected]
  • Web: https://www.mydatavalue.com/founding-ml-data-engineer/

jobs.title: Founding Engineer
jobs.summary: Job Description We're building the future of Revenue Management. AI agents that give short-term rental operators and independent hotels more money for less effort. We integrate directly with Booking.com , Airbnb, and PMS platforms via deep private API connections. The role: This is a flexible Hybrid role in London. You'll be the second engineering hire, working directly alongside Martin. Everything is on the table: backend systems, React frontend, Chrome extension, data pipelines, agent orchestr
jobs.full_offer_text: Job Description We're building the future of Revenue Management. AI agents that give short-term rental operators and independent hotels more money for less effort. We integrate directly with Booking.com , Airbnb, and PMS platforms via deep private API connections. The role: This is a flexible Hybrid role in London. You'll be the second engineering hire, working directly alongside Martin. Everything is on the table: backend systems, React frontend, Chrome extension, data pipelines, agent orchestration, and prompt engineering. You'll ship full features end-to-end, not tickets. We ship fast using Claude Code and AI-assisted development daily. If you're already running multiple PRs in parallel with AI tools, you'll feel right at home. What we offer: £80K–£90K base salary plus up to 1% equity (DOE). This is a founding role, you'll have real ownership of the product and the company. You'll shape technical direction, product decisions, and company culture from the ground floor. If you want to build something real with a small team that's already winning customers and growing fast, where your code ships to production the same day and your decisions shape the company then let's talk. Restrictions Telecommuting is OK No Agencies Please Requirements What we're looking for: Previous startup or founder experience, you've built something from zero and know what that demands. Very strong Python (Django/Celery) and React. Comfortable across the full stack, backend, frontend, infra, data. AI agent development, prompt engineering, or data pipeline experience is a strong plus. You need to be based in London or able to come to our Canary Wharf office 2 days a week. We can't sponsor visas. Stack: Django, Celery, PostgreSQL, React, Chrome Extensions, Claude Code. About the Company About us: We're pre-seed funded, ramen profitable at $300k ARR, with ~40+ clients and near-zero churn. The product works, customers love it, and we're growing fast. Our team is sub-5 people, based out of Canary Wharf, London. Martin (CTO/co-founder) is a previous founder with 10+ years in development. Miro (CEO/co-founder) had a previous Revenue Management company and is an expert in data. Contact Info Contact: Martin Dawson E-mail contact: [email protected] Web: https://www.mydatavalue.com/founding-full-stack-senior-engineer
jobs.full_offer_html:
Job Description

We're building the future of Revenue Management. AI agents that give short-term rental operators and independent hotels more money for less effort. We integrate directly with Booking.com, Airbnb, and PMS platforms via deep private API connections.

The role: This is a flexible Hybrid role in London. You'll be the second engineering hire, working directly alongside Martin. Everything is on the table: backend systems, React frontend, Chrome extension, data pipelines, agent orchestration, and prompt engineering. You'll ship full features end-to-end, not tickets. We ship fast using Claude Code and AI-assisted development daily. If you're already running multiple PRs in parallel with AI tools, you'll feel right at home.

What we offer: £80K–£90K base salary plus up to 1% equity (DOE). This is a founding role, you'll have real ownership of the product and the company. You'll shape technical direction, product decisions, and company culture from the ground floor.

If you want to build something real with a small team that's already winning customers and growing fast, where your code ships to production the same day and your decisions shape the company then let's talk.

Restrictions

  • Telecommuting is OK
  • No Agencies Please

Requirements

What we're looking for: Previous startup or founder experience, you've built something from zero and know what that demands. Very strong Python (Django/Celery) and React. Comfortable across the full stack, backend, frontend, infra, data. AI agent development, prompt engineering, or data pipeline experience is a strong plus. You need to be based in London or able to come to our Canary Wharf office 2 days a week. We can't sponsor visas.

Stack: Django, Celery, PostgreSQL, React, Chrome Extensions, Claude Code.

About the Company

About us: We're pre-seed funded, ramen profitable at $300k ARR, with ~40+ clients and near-zero churn. The product works, customers love it, and we're growing fast. Our team is sub-5 people, based out of Canary Wharf, London. Martin (CTO/co-founder) is a previous founder with 10+ years in development. Miro (CEO/co-founder) had a previous Revenue Management company and is an expert in data.

Contact Info

  • Contact: Martin Dawson
  • E-mail contact: [email protected]
  • Web: https://www.mydatavalue.com/founding-full-stack-senior-engineer
